If, at a juncture, dust coloured like the Bandhuka flower
(blood red) or like the collyrium powder goes up towards the Sun,
mankind will then be afflicted by innumerable diseases, whereas
white dust, at the time, betokens prosperity, peace and happiness to
mankind.

The combination of the rays of the Sun, clouds and wind,
appearing in the form of a rod, is called Danda, When the Dan da

Indications at Dawn and Twilight XXX 289
appears in the intermediate directions, harm would come to the
kings, while in the cardinal directions, it forebodes evil to BrShma-
pas, Ksatriyas, Vaisyas and Sudras respectively.
